text:
BOTTOM LINE — Small caps led, energy faded, breadth modestly positive. VIX closed at 16.6, down versus its 20-day moving average of 17.6, signaling contained equity vol despite lingering macro and geopolitical risk. SPY gained 0.39% while IWM rallied 0.93% as easing Middle East risk and AI-chip enthusiasm kept the tape risk-on but not euphoric. Cross-asset, Treasuries (TLT +0.55%) and the dollar (UUP +0.14%) firmed even as gold (GLD -0.76%) and oil (USO -1.14%) were hit, fitting a relief-rally regime with fading hedges and rotation into cyclicals and tech.

INDICES — VIX printed 16.6 with its 20-day average at 17.6. Daily performance ranked IWM +0.93%, QQQ +0.42%, SPY +0.39%, DIA +0.60%, with STYLE showing SMALL > LARGE by 0.51pp on a small=+0.93% versus large=+0.42% spread. The standout cross-asset move was USO -1.14% on easing Strait of Hormuz risk while TLT gained 0.55%. Neither SPY at +0.39% nor QQQ at +0.42% triggered any 200-day moving average breakdown flags.

BREADTH — Breadth was constructive with 63.5% of names up, advancers/decliners at 132/76, volume up/down at 1.05, and 20/50/200-day participation at 56.7%, 61.5%, and 65.4%. Mag7 median was -0.12% versus ex-Mag7 median +0.42%, narrow_leadership=False, SPY lagged the universe median by 0.03pp (0.39% vs 0.42%), mega_cap_tilt=False, and defensives_minus_cyclicals was a negligible +0.05pp.

VOLUME — Index volume breadth showed DIA and RSP at 1.1x and IJH at 1.0x versus an indices average of 0.90x, while QQQ ran light at 0.8x and IJR at 0.7x. SPY’s volume was roughly in-line by implication of an indices average at 0.90x and no explicit flag, while sector-wise XLE at 1.1x, XLK at 0.9x, and XLI at 0.8x contrasted with XLP, XLB, and XLY at 0.7x each, pointing to accumulation in cyclicals and tech with low-conviction bids in defensives.

SECTORS — XLV Health Care +1.17%, XLK Technology +1.00%, XLU Utilities +0.78%, XLI Industrials +0.73%, XLE Energy +0.61%, XLB Materials +0.54%, XLF Financials +0.41%, XLY Discretionary +0.40%, XLP Staples +0.17%, XLRE Real Estate +0.13%, XLC Comms -0.55%. Top Industries ETFs were CIBR +2.59% and ROBO +2.42%, while BOAT -2.55% and KWEB -2.61% anchored the bottom.

ROTATION — Money flowed into semis and broader tech with SOXX +2.41% and DRIV +2.34% alongside XLK +1.00%, while flows exited China internet and travel with KWEB -2.61% and PEJ at 0.3x volume, and communications lagged with XLC -0.55%. Defensives_minus_cyclicals at +0.05pp and the Semi 7 +3.04% versus the Mag 7 -0.05% frame the day as AI hardware leading.

THE 7s — Energy +0.08%, Power +1.50%, Semi +3.04%, Software +2.41%, Health +1.99%. Housing -0.35%, Insurance -0.42%, Old Metals -0.21%, Medtech +0.12%, Toll Road +0.30%. In Semi +3.04%, the strongest name sat in the AI-chip complex, while the weakest semi lagged but still posted a positive print; in Housing -0.35%, the worst builder underperformed while the best held flat, showing mild internal divergence.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is June 17, 2026, with CME probabilities at 100% hold, 0% cut, 0% hike, and the current FFR target at 3.50%–3.75%. Fed stance tally shows 6 hawks, 5 neutral, 4 doves, while USD liquidity stress printed 37/100 (Normal) with Reserve Scarcity at the 77th percentile as the most elevated component and ON-RRP a negligible $1.0B.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call volume ratio at 0.85 signals moderate call demand, while QQQ at 1.10 and IWM at 3.98 show heavy downside hedging in growth and small caps with positive skew across all three indices. SPY gamma flip sits at 751.0 versus spot implied in the mid-740s via max pain 740.0, so the index trades just below the main positive gamma zone, while VIX at 16.6 below its 20-day average of 17.6 and MOVE at 78.4 above its 20-day 74.6 highlight relatively calm equity vol but still-elevated bond vol.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil’s -1.14% drop and OVX at 76.0 alongside GLD -0.76%, GVZ 23.9, and TLT +0.55% show easing Middle East risk pulling out commodity hedges while supporting duration. The dollar’s UUP +0.14% gain with HYG nearly flat at +0.01% and HY OAS at the 14th percentile confirms risk appetite holding even as safe havens cheapen.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 62.8/100 sits in Greed territory. Biggest individual movers on the day were INTU +4.19% in The Software 7 basket, EWH +1.53% in Countries, and UNG -3.44% in Commodities. On 20-day momentum, best sector is XLK +12.59% and worst sector is XLU -1.80%, while best basket is the Semi group +24.57% and worst basket is Medtech -5.24%. Volume spikes in INTU at 3.4x with price up +4.19% and IBM 2.7x with price +0.34% indicate accumulation in select software and legacy tech following AI and cost-optimization headlines.

WATCH — INTU +4.19% merits attention given 3.4x volume, strong price action, and its role inside the Software basket where sentiment is inflecting off deeply negative YTD returns. IJS +0.87% with 10.9x volume and IJK +0.88% with 6.8x volume suggest active factor rotation into small-cap value and core growth, potentially pointing to sustained small-cap leadership. UNG -3.44% with 20-day +6.11% and group-worst daily performance raises the question of whether today was a shakeout or the start of a reversal in the nat-gas uptrend.
